myths.statement: An NRI cannot vote in Indian elections.
myths.explanation: Fact: NRIs can register as overseas voters and vote in person at their respective polling stations in India.

myths.statement: EVMs can be hacked via Bluetooth or Wi-Fi.
myths.explanation: Fact: Indian EVMs are standalone, non-networked machines with no wireless communication capabilities.

myths.statement: Voting is legally mandatory in India.
myths.explanation: Fact: Voting is a right and a civic duty, but it is not mandatory by law in India.

myths.statement: One can vote using only the Aadhar Card.
myths.explanation: Fact: While Aadhar is an ID proof, you MUST be registered in the electoral roll to vote.

myths.statement: The Election Commission can disqualify a candidate for MCC violations.
myths.explanation: Fact: The ECI has the authority to bar candidates from campaigning or disqualify them for severe Model Code of Conduct violations.

myths.statement: If NOTA gets the most votes, the election is canceled.
myths.explanation: Fact: Currently, even if NOTA receives the highest number of votes, the candidate with the next highest votes is declared the winner.
